children.label: A
children.text: the protection; of public health, safety, and property from extreme danger of adverse effects of coal mining practices;1So in original.1

children.label: i
children.text: have been degraded by the adverse effects of coal mining practices; and

children.label: ii
children.text: are adjacent to a site that has been or will be remediated under subparagraph (A);

children.label: A
children.text: the protection of public health and safety from adverse effects of coal mining practices;

children.label: i
children.text: have been degraded by the adverse effects of coal mining practices; and

children.label: ii
children.text: are adjacent to a site that has been or will be remediated under subparagraph (A); and

the restoration of land and water resources and the environment previously degraded by adverse effects of coal mining practices including measures for the conservation and development of soil, water (excluding channelization), woodland, fish and wildlife, recreation resources, and agricultural productivity.

Any State or Indian tribe not certified under may expend funds allocated to such State or Indian tribe in any year through the grants made available under paragraphs (1) and (5) of for the purpose of protecting, repairing, replacing, constructing, or enhancing facilities relating to water supply, including water distribution facilities and treatment plants, to replace water supplies adversely affected by coal mining practices.section 1240a(a) of this titlesection 1232(g) of this title

If the adverse effect on water supplies referred to in this subsection occurred both prior to and after , or as the case may be, the dates (and under the criteria) set forth under , shall not be construed to prohibit a State or Indian tribe referred to in paragraph (1) from using funds referred to in such paragraph for the purposes of this subsection if the State or Indian tribe determines that such adverse effects occurred predominantly prior to , or as the case may be, the dates (and under the criteria) set forth under .August 3, 1977August 3, 1977section 1232(g)(4)(B) of this titlesection 1234 of this titlesection 1232(g)(4)(B) of this title

children.heading: Inventory
children.label: c Inventory
children.text: For the purposes of assisting in the planning and evaluation of reclamation projects pursuant to , and assisting in making the certification referred to in , the Secretary shall maintain an inventory of eligible lands and waters pursuant to which meet the priorities stated in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection (a). Under standardized procedures established by the Secretary, States and Indian tribes with approved abandoned mine reclamation programs pursuant to may offer amendments, subject to the approval of the Secretary, to update the inventory as it applies to eligible lands and waters under the jurisdiction of such States or tribes. The Secretary shall provide such States and tribes with the financial and technical assistance necessary for the purpose of making inventory amendments. The Secretary shall compile and maintain an inventory for States and Indian lands in the case when a State or Indian tribe does not have an approved abandoned mine reclamation program pursuant to . On a regular basis, but not less than annually, the projects completed under this subchapter shall be so noted on the inventory under standardized procedures established by the Secretary.section 1235 of this titlesection 1240a(a) of this titlesection 1234 of this titlesection 1235 of this titlesection 1235 of this title
